pred_reason: SWC-101 is justified by multiple high-severity reentrancy vectors in external calls to ZRX and 1inch, while SWC-107 is supported by arithmetic overflow/underflow vulnerabilities in unguarded operations like balance subtractions. Evidence: Unprotected external calls (ZRX_EXCHANGE_ADDRESS.call, ONE_SPLIT_ADDRESS.swap) enable reentrancy into state-changing functions; Arithmetic operations (balanceAfter - balanceBefore) lack SafeMath protections and risk overflow/underflow

raw.pred_reason: SWC-101 is justified by multiple high-severity reentrancy vectors in external calls to ZRX and 1inch, while SWC-107 is supported by arithmetic overflow/underflow vulnerabilities in unguarded operations like balance subtractions. Evidence: Unprotected external calls (ZRX_EXCHANGE_ADDRESS.call, ONE_SPLIT_ADDRESS.swap) enable reentrancy into state-changing functions; Arithmetic operations (balanceAfter - balanceBefore) lack SafeMath protections and risk overflow/underflow
